title: uragoga pachyneura
side_effects: Uragoga pachyneura can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its potent alkaloid content. Ingesting excessive amounts can lead to stomach ulcers, diarrhea, and vomiting. Additionally, applying uragoga pachyneura extracts topically can cause skin irritation, redness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. It is essential to consult with a medical professional before using the plant to avoid any adverse health consequences.
